scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) events — both PRs are migrations that alter the `events` table. Alan adds `webhook_status` / `delivery_attempts`, and Sarah adds `delivery_state` / `processor_version`.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C) Sarah — she only works Monday, Wednesday, Friday, so Thursday March 13 is not a working day for her.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B) SAML SSO — Josh told Acme Corp it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly said SAML was not in scope for this phase and would add 3 weeks.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps, including **first milestone**, which used to be the activation trigger. So “completion” is now a different definition, not apples-to-apples with the old 60% completion rate.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — **staging-3**. It was created for “load testing” but the workspace says it was **never actually used for load testing**.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text:
B) All three were in migration batch-7, run on February 25.

The three billing-related tickets each list the same last migration batch: **batch-7, run 2026-02-25**. That’s the common thread, not plan type, signup month, or the docs bug.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text:
C) 1 — only webhook payload format; cursor pagination already has consensus between Sarah and Alan.

The meeting notes say there are **2 open questions**, but one is already resolved: **cursor vs offset pagination** has Sarah recommending cursor and Alan agreeing. The only remaining unresolved item is **webhook payload: nested vs flat**.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) src/billing/mapping.rs — Alan calls it “the rosetta stone file,” and it maps between plan, tier, and subscription.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B) CSV only — all 3 customers confirmed CSV is sufficient; PDF was never requested and Excel came from the sales rep, not the customer.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C) 0 edge case tests and 7 e2e tests — the workspace says edge case tests are 0% of 342, and end-to-end tests are 7 (2%).

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B) Long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth — Sarah’s synthesis was that you can keep the developer experience simple while still getting JWT benefits like rotation and revocation.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The gap is **no delivery confirmation mechanism**. Even if the endpoint returns 200, the system can’t verify the customer actually processed the webhook, so failures could stay hidden until invoices/reconciliation break later.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B) Takeshi at NovaTech in Tokyo — he was explicitly offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective, and the context says no one has asked the customer perspective yet.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C) About 2.75 hours per week — the workspace totals 1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h generic error messages, plus missed bugs from the skipped test.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text:
C) 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah's existing webhook signature module means the change is localized — about half a day

The workspace notes two explicit asks in the last two weeks, and says adding a new algorithm would be localized to Sarah’s existing signature module, so it’s a cheap change.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C) Feature 5 — it breaks even at 5 features, and is net positive from feature 6 onward. The workspace math shows: 5 features = 25 days either way, and 6 features = 27 days with refactor vs 30 days without.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B) The spec is ambiguous — “subscription start date” could mean either signup date (Jan 15, what Acme expects) or billing period start (Feb 1, what engineering built). It's a 1-line UI label change.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B) Annual billing requires proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch.

The workspace shows Billing Migration Phase 2 is already targeting March 31 and is blocked on reconciling 847 subscriptions, with no buffer. Adding annual billing would introduce additional billing logic work right into that same critical path.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text:
B) 12 minutes detection; root cause was a process gap — no testing against production-scale data with historical records

The incident log says Sarah’s monitoring alert fired at **09:12** after the dry-run started at **09:00**, so detection took **12 minutes**. The root cause was that the script **wasn’t tested against production-scale data with full historical records**, especially legacy subscriptions missing `plan_change_date`.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C) 95% utilization at baseline; during typical 1.5-2x spikes the combined 143-190 req/sec exceeds the 100 limit, causing 429 errors.
